primary_blockers.name: Materialized QA remains REVIEW
primary_blockers.evidence: Latest bounded batch checked 3, passed 2, failed 1; target_count 229; rows_found 94.

primary_blockers.name: Plex decision endpoint instability
primary_blockers.evidence: Some rows return HTTP 200; others return socket hang up or timeout.
primary_blockers.risk: Catalogue may appear in Plex but not reliably play.

primary_blockers.name: Incomplete Plex DB row coverage
primary_blockers.evidence: 229 materialized targets discovered but only 94 Plex DB rows found.
primary_blockers.risk: Plex indexing/metadata visibility is still incomplete.

primary_blockers.name: Process launch saturation under heavy work
primary_blockers.evidence: Full QA and broad mirror publisher previously caused command launch timeouts; bounded QA keeps launch after QA near 20 ms.
primary_blockers.risk: Unbounded local work can degrade Plex host responsiveness.

primary_blockers.name: Service context path visibility mismatch
primary_blockers.evidence: LocalSystem/service context cannot reliably dereference user-session S: rclone mount symlinks.
primary_blockers.risk: Wrong verification strategy can fail good paths or trigger loops.

review_questions: Is Plex decision endpoint testing the right go-live proof?
Should QA be decomposed into indexing, WebDAV/path, real playback canary, and decision proof stages?
Is materialized symlink/WebDAV still the right production architecture?
What is the highest leverage process launch saturation fix?
Are socket hangup failures more likely Plex probing, WebDAV bridge, rclone/VFS cache, upstream Real-Debrid latency, or media/container metadata?
